name: GPUI
description: Zed editor's in-house GPU-accelerated framework, opened up for external use

license.comment: Published on crates.io as Apache-2.0. The Zed repo hosts multiple LICENSE files (AGPL for Zed itself, Apache and GPL alongside) because individual crates carry their own licenses; the gpui crate is Apache-2.0.

documentation.comment: Official guide site; API docs also on docs.rs/gpui. README notes documentation is still under development.

latest-stable-version.comment: Pre-1.0; README states breaking changes are expected between versions.

maintenance-status.comment: Developed alongside Zed; regular releases (0.2.0/0.2.1/0.2.2 all in October 2025).

maturity.comment: Explicitly pre-1.0 per the README: "still in active development as we work on the Zed code editor, and is still pre-1.0. There will often be breaking changes between versions." Production-proven via Zed, but unstable API.

implementation.comment: No runtime dependency on a C/C++ toolkit; renders via wgpu (Linux/Windows) or Metal via objc2 bindings (macOS). Platform glue is considered pure-Rust in this comparison.

rendering-mode.comment: README: "a hybrid immediate and retained mode, GPU accelerated, UI framework for Rust." Views rebuild element trees each frame from state while an entity graph retains scoped state.

reactivity-model.comment: Views implement a Render trait returning element trees (react-like rebuild-and-diff). Interactivity is callback-driven (on_click, on_mouse_down, etc.). State ownership is via entities/models in a central AppContext rather than fine-grained signals.

thread-model.comment: UI runs on the platform main thread; GPUI provides a BackgroundExecutor for off-thread async work that can dispatch back to the UI.

renderer.comment: macOS renders with Metal directly. Linux and Windows use wgpu, which in turn targets Vulkan (Linux) and DirectX 12 (Windows). Shader code is WGSL and portable across all three.

hidpi-support.comment: GPUI performs pixel-aligned rounding for HiDPI and subpixel glyph positioning; Zed ships on Retina displays.

vector-rendering.comment: Paths, gradients and SVG are supported; examples directory includes painting.rs, gradient.rs, paths_bench.rs and an svg/ example.

custom-shaders.comment: No public custom-shader/render-pass API documented. GPUI's WGSL pipelines (quads, shadows, sprites, paths) are internal. Could not confirm a stable hook for app-supplied shaders.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: No documented API for embedding an external wgpu/OpenGL viewport inside the UI. Apps can add custom Element types that paint via the built-in Scene primitives, but surfacing a user-owned render pass isn't advertised.

windows.comment: Windows support shipped as a public beta of Zed in late 2025 (Windows 10 1809+ required, Vulkan-capable GPU). Renders via wgpu→DirectX 12. Still newer than macOS/Linux; some Windows-specific integrations have rough edges.

macos.comment: Primary development target. Renders via Metal. Requires Xcode.

linux-x11.comment: x11 is a default Cargo feature of the gpui crate.

linux-wayland.comment: wayland is a default Cargo feature; Zed picks between X11 and Wayland at runtime via guess_compositor.

ios.comment: No official iOS support. A community fork (gpui-mobile) implements the Platform trait for iOS via wgpu/Metal, but this isn't maintained by Zed Industries.

android.comment: Same as iOS: only unofficial community fork via wgpu/Vulkan.

web-wasm.comment: A wasm32-unknown-unknown platform stub has been prototyped (see PR #50228 and discussion #8203) but is not usable for real apps; not officially supported.

embedded-no-std.comment: Not designed for no_std or microcontrollers; depends on std, wgpu, cosmic-text, and platform windowing.

authoring-style.comment: Views are written as Rust code that builds element trees via chained builder methods (div().flex().bg(...).child(...)). No DSL, RSX, or XML in the core framework; community crates like gpux add JSX-style macros on top.

hot-reload.comment: No first-class hot reload. A demo project shows reloading layout/styles from JSON files at runtime, but general UI changes still require a recompile.

layout-system.comment: Layout is delegated to Taffy, which supports both CSS Flexbox and CSS Grid.

styling-approach.comment: README describes a "tailwind-style API": styles are applied by chaining methods inline on elements (e.g., .bg_red_500().p_2()). Themes are expressed as Rust structs consumed through a Theme API.

theme-switching.comment: Zed itself supports runtime theme switching between light/dark and user-defined themes, built on GPUI's theme API. Community crates like gpui-component expose multi-theme support.

accessibility.comment: No AccessKit integration and no native a11y bridge. Zed's own discussion (active through October 2025) acknowledges "zero practical accessibility with screen readers" outside of system-provided dialogs, and a11y is described as a long-term initiative beyond 1.0.

internationalization.comment: No built-in i18n/RTL support. Proposals and RFCs for localization in Zed are open but not implemented; community crates (e.g., yororen-ui) layer JSON translation loading on top.

widget-breadth.comment: Core GPUI ships primitives (div, text, list, uniform_list, tree, popover) but not buttons, inputs, dropdowns, dialogs, or form widgets — those come from third-party component libraries (gpui-component, adabraka-ui, yororen-ui).

data-grid-table.comment: UniformList provides O(1) virtualized layout for fixed-height rows and List provides O(log N) for variable-height rows via SumTree indexing; examples include data_table.rs. A polished table widget is not part of core.

rich-text-markdown.comment: Core GPUI renders plain text runs with styling; there's no markdown widget in the public crate. Zed's markdown rendering lives in separate internal crates.

tree-outline.comment: Examples directory includes tree.rs demonstrating a hierarchical tree widget.

native-menus.comment: set_menus.rs example shows platform-native menu bar integration.

file-dialogs.comment: Platform trait exposes native open/save file dialogs; Zed uses them for workspace picking.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party charting widget. Third-party plotters-gpui provides a plotters backend.

custom-widgets.comment: The Element trait is well-documented and views compose freely, but custom low-level elements need some understanding of the three-phase (layout/prepaint/paint) pipeline. Not trivial, not arcane.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: GPUI ships its own BackgroundExecutor/ForegroundExecutor integrated with the platform event loop. External runtimes like tokio aren't natively integrated; users typically use GPUI's executor.

system-tray.comment: Not provided by core. A third-party fork (adabraka-gpui) adds tray and global-hotkey support.

notifications.comment: No built-in OS notification API in core GPUI; same adabraka fork adds native notifications.

clipboard.comment: Platform trait exposes clipboard read/write; x11-clipboard is pulled in as part of the default x11 feature set.

drag-and-drop.comment: Supports both in-window drag-and-drop and OS-level file drops; drag_drop.rs example demonstrates both paths.

embedded-webview.comment: No built-in webview element. Could be added via wry in user code, but not part of the framework.

multi-window.comment: App::open_window can be called multiple times; window.rs and window_positioning.rs examples demonstrate multi-window usage.

binary-size.comment: Not measured in this pass. No credible published "hello world" size measurement for a minimal GPUI app stripped and released; Zed the full editor binary is ~50MB but is not representative.

startup-time.comment: Not measured in this pass. GPUI targets 120fps rendering and sub-millisecond input latency but cold-start figures for a minimal app have not been reliably benchmarked.

idle-redraw.comment: Framework intelligently determines which parts of the view need to be redrawn in response to state changes; does not continuously repaint when idle. 120fps target applies to active rendering, not idle.

dependency-count.comment: ~60 direct dependencies with wgpu, cosmic-text, wayland-*, x11rb and friends pulled in by default; transitive graph comfortably exceeds 200 crates for a default build.

documentation-quality.comment: README explicitly says "the best way to learn about these APIs is to read the Zed source code or drop a question in the Zed Discord." The gpui.rs site and docs.rs exist but a full book/tutorial set is still under development.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust with builder-style chaining, derive macros for actions, and entity handles that integrate with the borrow checker. Some friction around Context/Entity lifetimes and the three-phase render pipeline when writing custom elements.

examples-count.comment: 36+ example .rs files plus image/ and svg/ subdirectories in crates/gpui/examples (hello_world, input, tree, data_table, drag_drop, animation, text_layout, uniform_list, etc.).

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground; framework is native-desktop only.

ide-tooling.comment: `inspector` is a Cargo feature of the gpui crate that enables a runtime widget inspector. No custom LSP (not needed — rust-analyzer covers the Rust-only API). Syntax highlighting comes via standard Rust tooling.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: An Inspector type and `inspector` feature flag expose a runtime element-tree inspector for debugging layout and styles.

github-stars.comment: Snapshot of Zed repository stars on 2026-04-24 (~79.7k). GPUI does not have its own repo; it lives in crates/gpui inside zed-industries/zed. Star count reflects the broader Zed project.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads per crates.io listing; figure is an October 2025 snapshot and has since grown (lib.rs reported ~18k/month in April 2026). Values below 100k reflect that GPUI was only published standalone in 2022 and that Zed itself uses the source, not the published crate.

last-release.comment: v0.2.2 released 2025-10-22; v0.2.0 and v0.2.1 also published in October 2025.

notable-production-users.value: Zed editor (Zed Industries); community apps include Loungy (Spotlight-style launcher), DBFlux (multi-DB client), Fulgur (editor), GitComet (Git UI), hummingbird (music player), Fast Forward (macOS window switcher)

community-size.comment: No dedicated GPUI chat; the Zed Discord and zed-industries GitHub discussions serve as the primary channels. The awesome-gpui list shows a small-but-growing set of third-party apps and libraries (~20 entries).

ecosystem-crates.comment: Ecosystem includes gpui-component (60+ widgets), adabraka-ui, yororen-ui, gpui-hooks, gpui-router, gpui-video-player, gpui-storybook, plotters-gpui, gpux (JSX+Tailwind macro layer), GPUI-RSX. Still far from the breadth of iced/egui ecosystems.
